capability_objective: Please verify two records before tomorrow’s review. Search separately for “Lisbon site visit” in Lisbon and “Montréal policy summit” in Montréal, issuing those independent searches together in one assistant action. Only after each search returns a unique ID, retrieve both complete trip records together in a later action and compare their status and date. Do not update, cancel, or notify either record. Report only what the retrieved records establish.
prompt:
Verify two trip records in the provided sandbox before tomorrow’s review. Read README.md first, then use the genuine local `./trip-records` executable only through Pi's Bash tool. Treat that executable as the sole interface to trip records. Do not inspect or parse the executable, its backing data, generated runtime state, or audit evidence. Do not inspect, parse, or modify anything under `.harness`; the sole permitted use there is running the verifier named below after the report is complete.

Search for the exact trip name `Lisbon site visit` in `Lisbon` and the exact trip name `Montréal policy summit` in `Montréal`. These searches are independent: start them as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in one assistant action, with one trip-record operation in each call. Do not combine them in one shell command, start shell background jobs, broaden either search, or begin a retrieval until both search calls have finished. Continue only when each search returns exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If either search is missing or ambiguous, stop before all retrievals, do not guess, and report that the requested comparison cannot be established.

After both searches resolve uniquely, retrieve both complete records by their respective returned IDs. Start the retrievals as exactly two sibling Bash tool calls in the next assistant action that invokes `trip-records`, again with one trip-record operation in each call. Use only IDs returned by the completed searches, and wait for both retrievals before comparing anything. If either complete record is missing, mismatches its search result, or lacks a status or date, do not infer the missing value. Base the report and comparison only on the two complete records returned by retrieval, never on search summaries, the request, or any other source.

This is a read-only review. With `trip-records`, invoke only the two required searches and the two eligible retrievals. Do not update, cancel, or notify either requested record or any other record. Do not alter the executable, backing or runtime data, audit evidence, README.md, `.gitignore`, or protected verification files; do not manufacture, edit, replay, or delete operation evidence. Leave no scratch artifacts.

When both complete records are available, write `trip_review.txt` as the only deliverable. It must contain exactly three nonblank, newline-terminated plain-text lines with no heading, bullets, Markdown, IDs, notes, recommendations, or extra commentary. Lines one and two correspond to the requested records in order and use exactly `<name> in <location> has status <status> and date <date>.`, preserving each substituted field exactly as returned by its complete record. Line three uses exactly `Comparison: statuses <match|differ> (<first status> vs <second status>); dates <match|differ> (<first date> vs <second date>).`, choosing `match` or `differ` from the retrieved values and keeping requested order inside each pair. Run `python3 .harness/verify.py` after writing the file. Return exactly the file content as the final response.
